Le Dalou

Brasserie · Seafood · Place de la Nation

A classic Parisian brasserie on Place de la Nation, known for fresh seafood delivered daily and a heated terrace looking out over the square.

Welcome

Le Dalou has held its corner of Place de la Nation for decades, serving the kind of brasserie fare Parisians grew up with: oysters and seafood platters, classic fish dishes, and a green-and-brass dining room that hasn't chased any trends. Whether you're stopping for a quick coffee or settling in for a long seafood lunch, the terrace puts you right in the middle of one of the 12th arrondissement's busiest squares.

At a Glance

About Le Dalou

A Parisian brasserie, done properly

The interior photo above shows Brasserie Julien, a different Paris brasserie, used here to set the mood — Le Dalou's own interior photos aren't available under a license we can reuse. Stop by in person, or check the review sites linked on the Contact section, to see the real room.

The Room

Le Dalou keeps the look of a traditional French brasserie: dark green tones, brass fittings, mirrors, and tightly packed tables that fill up fast at lunch. A covered, heated terrace wraps around the front, putting diners right at the edge of Place de la Nation — useful for people-watching, and for the regulars who treat it as their morning coffee spot.

The Kitchen

The kitchen leans on seafood, with product brought in fresh from Saint-Malo. Expect platters of oysters and shellfish alongside the usual brasserie staples — steak frites, sole, onion soup — for anyone not in a seafood mood.

The Square

Place de la Nation is one of the larger squares in eastern Paris, anchored by the "Triomphe de la République" statue and ringed by grand 19th-century buildings. It sits at the meeting point of the 11th and 12th arrondissements, with its own Métro station making it an easy stop on the way through the city.

Background photo: Coyau / Wikimedia Commons, CC BY-SA 3.0
